items.headline: City addresses affordable housing protections if subsidies expire
items.summary: The council discussed new affordability restrictions to protect low-income residents if project-based housing subsidy contracts end or properties face foreclosure. This could affect long-term housing stability for vulnerable residents in San José.

items.headline: City Council Reviews $2027 Capital Improvement Budget Plans
items.summary: The council held a study session to examine the fiscal year 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program, which outlines planned infrastructure and development projects for Mountain View. This is worth tracking if you're interested in what physical improvements or construction projects the city is prioritizing next year.

items.headline: City Council introduces changes to tenant protection rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is amending its Residential Tenant Protections Program ordinance, which could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in the city. The specific changes were not detailed in the agenda, but this represents a potential shift in how tenant-landlord relationships are regulated locally.

items.headline: City updates plans for youth services and library facilities
items.summary: San José presented status reports on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan and Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan. These reports indicate the city is evaluating and potentially updating how it serves young people and manages public library spaces.

items.headline: Downtown historic building gets floor area bonus for renovation
items.summary: The City Council is considering elevating 405 Kipling Street from a Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource, which would allow the owner to add 5,000 sq ft of bonus floor area in exchange for historic and seismic rehabilitation work. This incentivizes preserving an older downtown building while allowing expansion.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed on San Antonio Road
items.summary: A developer is seeking to rezone and redevelop two properties on San Antonio Road into an eight-story building with 167 residential units (28 affordable) and 1,400 sq ft of commercial space. The project requires City Council approval and represents significant new housing near the service commercial corridor.

items.headline: 333 Franklin Street zoning hearing postponed indefinitely
items.summary: A zoning hearing for a project at 333 Franklin Street has been cancelled with no new date set. Residents expecting to weigh in on this development should watch for a new hearing notice.

items.headline: City Council authorizes construction contract with QLM, Inc.
items.summary: The Town Manager was given authority to execute a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the agenda summary does not specify which project this covers. Residents may want to check the full meeting minutes to understand what construction work this contract will fund.
